title: Postmortem INC-2101: Billing run produced duplicate invoices
text:
# Postmortem INC-2101: Billing run produced duplicate invoices

**Date:** 2025-07-16 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Nico Robin (Payments & Billing)

The following is the incident review for INC-2101, billing run produced duplicate invoices. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

A subset of customers and internal workflows were affected. Time to detect was 6 minutes; time to mitigate was 29 minutes; full resolution took 248 minutes.

## Affected services

- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans

## Responders

- Nico Robin (Payments & Billing) — incident lead
- Light Yagami (Payments & Billing) — responder
- Olivier Armstrong (Data Platform) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to billing-engine.
- **T+6m** — Nico Robin was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+29m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+248m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the invoice job lacked an idempotency key, so a retried batch re-emitted invoices that had already been written.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Add idempotency keys to all batch write jobs (owner: Nico Robin)
- [ ] Alert on duplicate invoice numbers (owner: Nico Robin)

title: Postmortem INC-2102: Payment gateway timeout spike during evening peak
text:
# Postmortem INC-2102: Payment gateway timeout spike during evening peak

**Date:** 2025-08-05 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing)

The following is the incident review for INC-2102, payment gateway timeout spike during evening peak. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

The incident was visible to customers and triggered inbound support contacts. Time to detect was 6 minutes; time to mitigate was 95 minutes; full resolution took 147 minutes.

## Affected services

- **payment-gateway** (owned by Payments & Billing): processes card and bank payments with external PSPs
- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans

## Responders

- Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing) — incident lead
- Nico Robin (Payments & Billing) — responder
- Misato Katsuragi (Core Infrastructure) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to payment-gateway.
- **T+6m** — Roronoa Zoro was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+95m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+147m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the HTTP connection pool to the external PSP was capped at 20 connections, which saturated during the 19:00 payment peak and queued requests until they timed out.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Raise PSP connection pool to 200 with queue rejection (owner: Roronoa Zoro)
- [ ] Add pool saturation dashboards (owner: Roronoa Zoro)

title: Postmortem INC-2103: Customer portal login failures after auth deploy
text:
# Postmortem INC-2103: Customer portal login failures after auth deploy

**Date:** 2025-08-20 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Nami (Customer Platform)

This postmortem covers customer portal login failures after auth deploy. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Customer-facing functionality was degraded for a significant share of users. Time to detect was 10 minutes; time to mitigate was 94 minutes; full resolution took 278 minutes.

## Affected services

- **customer-portal** (owned by Customer Platform): customer-facing web application for accounts and usage
- **auth-service** (owned by Security & Identity): authentication and token issuance for all platforms

## Responders

- Nami (Customer Platform) — incident lead
- Hinata Hyuga (Security & Identity) — responder
- Kakashi Hatake (Core Infrastructure) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to customer-portal.
- **T+10m** — Nami was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+94m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+278m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the new signing key was rotated in auth-service before customer-portal had picked up the updated JWKS, so token validation failed for freshly issued tokens.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Automate JWKS propagation checks before key rotation (owner: Nami)
- [ ] Add canary token validation to deploys (owner: Kakashi Hatake)

title: Postmortem INC-2104: Telemetry ingest lag exceeding 15 minutes
text:
# Postmortem INC-2104: Telemetry ingest lag exceeding 15 minutes

**Date:** 2025-09-04 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Motoko Kusanagi (Grid Operations)

The following is the incident review for INC-2104, telemetry ingest lag exceeding 15 minutes. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

Impact was significant internally but customer visibility was limited. Time to detect was 25 minutes; time to mitigate was 114 minutes; full resolution took 251 minutes.

## Affected services

- **telemetry-ingest** (owned by Grid Operations): ingests real-time sensor data from grid substations
- **grid-monitor** (owned by Grid Operations): real-time monitoring and alerting for grid health

## Responders

- Motoko Kusanagi (Grid Operations) — incident lead
- Spike Spiegel (Grid Operations) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to telemetry-ingest.
- **T+25m** — Motoko Kusanagi was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+114m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+251m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that a consumer group rebalance left two Kafka partitions unassigned for 40 minutes, so substation events accumulated unprocessed.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Dashboards made the blast radius clear quickly.

## Action items

- [ ] Alert on unassigned partitions within 5 minutes (owner: Spike Spiegel)
- [ ] Document consumer rebalance runbook (owner: Motoko Kusanagi)

title: Postmortem INC-2105: Smart meter readings missing for region North
text:
# Postmortem INC-2105: Smart meter readings missing for region North

**Date:** 2025-09-19 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Winry Rockbell (Field Systems)

This postmortem covers smart meter readings missing for region North. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

A subset of customers and internal workflows were affected. Time to detect was 25 minutes; time to mitigate was 99 minutes; full resolution took 216 minutes.

## Affected services

- **meter-reader** (owned by Field Systems): collects readings from smart meters in the field
- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans

## Responders

- Winry Rockbell (Field Systems) — incident lead
- Shinji Ikari (Field Systems) — responder
- Mikasa Ackerman (Payments & Billing) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to meter-reader.
- **T+25m** — Winry Rockbell was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+99m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+216m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that a firmware update changed the meter payload encoding from CBOR to a proprietary framing that meter-reader could not parse, and unparseable readings were dropped silently.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Add parse-failure metrics with paging alert (owner: Winry Rockbell)
- [ ] Require decoding contract tests for firmware rollouts (owner: Shinji Ikari)

title: Postmortem INC-2106: Forecast service producing negative demand values
text:
# Postmortem INC-2106: Forecast service producing negative demand values

**Date:** 2025-10-04 · **Severity:** SEV3 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Senku Ishigami (Forecasting & AI)

This postmortem covers forecast service producing negative demand values. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Impact was contained to internal teams; no customer-facing degradation was observed. Time to detect was 5 minutes; time to mitigate was 73 minutes; full resolution took 127 minutes.

## Affected services

- **forecast-service** (owned by Forecasting & AI): predicts energy demand using ML models

## Responders

- Senku Ishigami (Forecasting & AI) — incident lead
- Frieren (Forecasting & AI) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to forecast-service.
- **T+5m** — Senku Ishigami was paged and declared SEV3. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+73m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+127m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that a data pipeline change introduced negative consumption rows into the training set, and the model learned to extrapolate them.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Dashboards made the blast radius clear quickly.

## Action items

- [ ] Add range validation to training pipeline (owner: Frieren)
- [ ] Reject models whose predictions violate physical bounds (owner: Senku Ishigami)

title: Postmortem INC-2107: API gateway 502 errors under load test
text:
# Postmortem INC-2107: API gateway 502 errors under load test

**Date:** 2025-10-14 · **Severity:** SEV3 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Franky (Core Infrastructure)

The following is the incident review for INC-2107, aPI gateway 502 errors under load test. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

Impact was contained to internal teams; no customer-facing degradation was observed. Time to detect was 17 minutes; time to mitigate was 105 minutes; full resolution took 166 minutes.

## Affected services

- **api-gateway** (owned by Core Infrastructure): edge routing, rate limiting and TLS termination

## Responders

- Franky (Core Infrastructure) — incident lead
- Yor Forger (Core Infrastructure) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to api-gateway.
- **T+17m** — Franky was paged and declared SEV3. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+105m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+166m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that upstream keepalive timeout (60s) exceeded the load balancer idle timeout (30s), so the LB reused connections the upstream had already closed.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Align LB and upstream keepalive timeouts (owner: Yor Forger)
- [ ] Add 502 rate to golden signals (owner: Yor Forger)

title: Postmortem INC-2108: Outage notifications sent to wrong customers
text:
# Postmortem INC-2108: Outage notifications sent to wrong customers

**Date:** 2025-10-29 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Usopp (Customer Platform)

The following is the incident review for INC-2108, outage notifications sent to wrong customers. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

Customer-facing functionality was degraded for a significant share of users. Time to detect was 9 minutes; time to mitigate was 37 minutes; full resolution took 78 minutes.

## Affected services

- **outage-notifier** (owned by Customer Platform): sends outage notifications via email, SMS and push
- **customer-portal** (owned by Customer Platform): customer-facing web application for accounts and usage

## Responders

- Usopp (Customer Platform) — incident lead
- Tanjiro Kamado (Customer Platform) — responder
- Nami (Customer Platform) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to outage-notifier.
- **T+9m** — Usopp was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+37m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+78m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the notification recipient cache was keyed by postal code instead of account id, so customers in the same postal code received each other's outage notifications.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Dashboards made the blast radius clear quickly.

## Action items

- [ ] Key all per-customer caches by account id (owner: Usopp)
- [ ] Add PII-safety review to notification changes (owner: Usopp)

title: Postmortem INC-2109: Data lake sync silently dropping meter events
text:
# Postmortem INC-2109: Data lake sync silently dropping meter events

**Date:** 2025-11-13 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** L Lawliet (Data Platform)

This postmortem covers data lake sync silently dropping meter events. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

A subset of customers and internal workflows were affected. Time to detect was 15 minutes; time to mitigate was 70 minutes; full resolution took 216 minutes.

## Affected services

- **data-lake-sync** (owned by Data Platform): syncs operational data into the analytics lake
- **meter-reader** (owned by Field Systems): collects readings from smart meters in the field

## Responders

- L Lawliet (Data Platform) — incident lead
- Bulma (Data Platform) — responder
- Loid Forger (Field Systems) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to data-lake-sync.
- **T+15m** — L Lawliet was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+70m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+216m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that meter-reader added a new field to its event schema; data-lake-sync's strict Avro reader rejected the events and the dead-letter queue had no alerting.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Move to schema registry with compatibility checks (owner: Bulma)
- [ ] Alert on dead-letter queue depth (owner: Bulma)

title: Postmortem INC-2110: Tariff update applied retroactively to closed invoices
text:
# Postmortem INC-2110: Tariff update applied retroactively to closed invoices

**Date:** 2025-11-28 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Mikasa Ackerman (Payments & Billing)

This postmortem covers tariff update applied retroactively to closed invoices. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Impact was significant internally but customer visibility was limited. Time to detect was 24 minutes; time to mitigate was 78 minutes; full resolution took 287 minutes.

## Affected services

- **tariff-service** (owned by Payments & Billing): manages tariff plans and pricing rules
- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans

## Responders

- Mikasa Ackerman (Payments & Billing) — incident lead
- Light Yagami (Payments & Billing) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to tariff-service.
- **T+24m** — Mikasa Ackerman was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+78m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+287m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the tariff update API defaulted effective_date to 1970-01-01 when the field was omitted, causing recalculation of historical invoices.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Make effective_date mandatory in tariff API (owner: Mikasa Ackerman)
- [ ] Guard recalculation of settled invoices (owner: Mikasa Ackerman)

title: Postmortem INC-2111: Auth token validation latency degrading all platforms
text:
# Postmortem INC-2111: Auth token validation latency degrading all platforms

**Date:** 2025-12-13 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Erza Scarlet (Security & Identity)

This postmortem covers auth token validation latency degrading all platforms. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Customer-facing functionality was degraded for a significant share of users. Time to detect was 17 minutes; time to mitigate was 85 minutes; full resolution took 184 minutes.

## Affected services

- **auth-service** (owned by Security & Identity): authentication and token issuance for all platforms
- **api-gateway** (owned by Core Infrastructure): edge routing, rate limiting and TLS termination
- **mobile-api** (owned by Customer Platform): backend-for-frontend serving the mobile apps

## Responders

- Erza Scarlet (Security & Identity) — incident lead
- Hinata Hyuga (Security & Identity) — responder
- Misato Katsuragi (Core Infrastructure) — responder
- Franky (Core Infrastructure) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to auth-service.
- **T+17m** — Erza Scarlet was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+85m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+184m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that an intermediate certificate expired; token validation fell back to full chain resolution on every request, adding 800ms of latency platform-wide.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Monitor certificate expiry at 30/14/7 days (owner: Misato Katsuragi)
- [ ] Cache full chain validation results (owner: Erza Scarlet)

title: Postmortem INC-2112: Grid monitor false alarms flooding on-call
text:
# Postmortem INC-2112: Grid monitor false alarms flooding on-call

**Date:** 2025-12-23 · **Severity:** SEV3 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Spike Spiegel (Grid Operations)

This postmortem covers grid monitor false alarms flooding on-call. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Impact was contained to internal teams; no customer-facing degradation was observed. Time to detect was 4 minutes; time to mitigate was 64 minutes; full resolution took 196 minutes.

## Affected services

- **grid-monitor** (owned by Grid Operations): real-time monitoring and alerting for grid health

## Responders

- Spike Spiegel (Grid Operations) — incident lead
- Edward Elric (Grid Operations) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to grid-monitor.
- **T+4m** — Spike Spiegel was paged and declared SEV3. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+64m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+196m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that a sensitivity threshold was accidentally deployed with the staging value (0.1 instead of 0.7), turning normal fluctuation into alerts.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Separate staging and production config repositories (owner: Spike Spiegel)
- [ ] Add config diff to deploy approval (owner: Edward Elric)

title: Postmortem INC-2113: Payment settlement file rejected by bank
text:
# Postmortem INC-2113: Payment settlement file rejected by bank

**Date:** 2026-01-02 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing)

This postmortem covers payment settlement file rejected by bank. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

A subset of customers and internal workflows were affected. Time to detect was 23 minutes; time to mitigate was 106 minutes; full resolution took 237 minutes.

## Affected services

- **payment-gateway** (owned by Payments & Billing): processes card and bank payments with external PSPs

## Responders

- Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing) — incident lead
- Lelouch Lamperouge (Security & Identity) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to payment-gateway.
- **T+23m** — Roronoa Zoro was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+106m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+237m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the bank switched settlement format from fixed-width to ISO 20022 XML on a contractual date that was tracked in a spreadsheet nobody checked.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Track partner contract dates in the service catalog (owner: Lelouch Lamperouge)
- [ ] Add settlement file schema validation in CI (owner: Roronoa Zoro)

title: Postmortem INC-2114: Mobile app showing stale usage data
text:
# Postmortem INC-2114: Mobile app showing stale usage data

**Date:** 2026-01-12 · **Severity:** SEV3 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Asuka Langley (Customer Platform)

This postmortem covers mobile app showing stale usage data. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Impact was contained to internal teams; no customer-facing degradation was observed. Time to detect was 20 minutes; time to mitigate was 108 minutes; full resolution took 205 minutes.

## Affected services

- **mobile-api** (owned by Customer Platform): backend-for-frontend serving the mobile apps
- **data-lake-sync** (owned by Data Platform): syncs operational data into the analytics lake

## Responders

- Asuka Langley (Customer Platform) — incident lead
- Shikamaru Nara (Data Platform) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to mobile-api.
- **T+20m** — Asuka Langley was paged and declared SEV3. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+108m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+205m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that usage-summary cache TTL was raised from 5 minutes to 24 hours during a cost-cutting change, without invalidation on new data arrival.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Invalidate usage cache on ingestion events (owner: Shikamaru Nara)
- [ ] Require review for TTL changes above 1 hour (owner: Shikamaru Nara)

title: Postmortem INC-2115: Dispatch optimizer using outdated forecasts
text:
# Postmortem INC-2115: Dispatch optimizer using outdated forecasts

**Date:** 2026-01-27 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Levi Ackerman (Grid Operations)

This postmortem covers dispatch optimizer using outdated forecasts. It is written blamelessly; our goal is to improve the system, not to assign fault.

## Impact

Impact was significant internally but customer visibility was limited. Time to detect was 7 minutes; time to mitigate was 92 minutes; full resolution took 248 minutes.

## Affected services

- **dispatch-optimizer** (owned by Grid Operations): optimizes power dispatch across generation assets
- **forecast-service** (owned by Forecasting & AI): predicts energy demand using ML models

## Responders

- Levi Ackerman (Grid Operations) — incident lead
- Kurisu Makise (Forecasting & AI) — responder
- Frieren (Forecasting & AI) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to dispatch-optimizer.
- **T+7m** — Levi Ackerman was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+92m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+248m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the forecast publication step ran before model inference completed, so the optimizer consumed the previous day's forecast file.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Add explicit dependency between inference and publication steps (owner: Levi Ackerman)
- [ ] Fail closed when forecast file is stale (owner: Levi Ackerman)

title: Postmortem INC-2116: Reporting service regulatory export failed month-end
text:
# Postmortem INC-2116: Reporting service regulatory export failed month-end

**Date:** 2026-02-11 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Bulma (Data Platform)

The following is the incident review for INC-2116, reporting service regulatory export failed month-end. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

Impact was significant internally but customer visibility was limited. Time to detect was 8 minutes; time to mitigate was 82 minutes; full resolution took 264 minutes.

## Affected services

- **reporting-service** (owned by Data Platform): generates regulatory and internal reports
- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans

## Responders

- Bulma (Data Platform) — incident lead
- Olivier Armstrong (Data Platform) — responder
- Nico Robin (Payments & Billing) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to reporting-service.
- **T+8m** — Bulma was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+82m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+264m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the month-end export query joined twelve months of invoice lines without an index on billing_period, exceeding the 300s statement timeout.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Dashboards made the blast radius clear quickly.

## Action items

- [ ] Add billing_period index (owner: Olivier Armstrong)
- [ ] Stream month-end export in batches (owner: Nico Robin)

title: Postmortem INC-2117: Customer portal checkout errors for corporate accounts
text:
# Postmortem INC-2117: Customer portal checkout errors for corporate accounts

**Date:** 2026-02-26 · **Severity:** SEV2 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Tanjiro Kamado (Customer Platform)

On 2026-02-26, NordWind experienced customer portal checkout errors for corporate accounts. This document records the timeline, root cause, and follow-up actions.

## Impact

Impact was significant internally but customer visibility was limited. Time to detect was 19 minutes; time to mitigate was 71 minutes; full resolution took 242 minutes.

## Affected services

- **customer-portal** (owned by Customer Platform): customer-facing web application for accounts and usage
- **payment-gateway** (owned by Payments & Billing): processes card and bank payments with external PSPs

## Responders

- Tanjiro Kamado (Customer Platform) — incident lead
- Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to customer-portal.
- **T+19m** — Tanjiro Kamado was paged and declared SEV2. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+71m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+242m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that corporate invoices above €50,000 exceeded the PSP's per-transaction limit; the gateway surfaced this as a generic 402 the portal did not handle.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Split corporate payments above PSP limit (owner: Tanjiro Kamado)
- [ ] Map PSP error codes to actionable portal messages (owner: Roronoa Zoro)

title: Postmortem INC-2118: Meter reader fleet disconnects after network change
text:
# Postmortem INC-2118: Meter reader fleet disconnects after network change

**Date:** 2026-03-13 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Winry Rockbell (Field Systems)

The following is the incident review for INC-2118, meter reader fleet disconnects after network change. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

The incident was visible to customers and triggered inbound support contacts. Time to detect was 13 minutes; time to mitigate was 47 minutes; full resolution took 152 minutes.

## Affected services

- **meter-reader** (owned by Field Systems): collects readings from smart meters in the field
- **telemetry-ingest** (owned by Grid Operations): ingests real-time sensor data from grid substations

## Responders

- Winry Rockbell (Field Systems) — incident lead
- Yor Forger (Core Infrastructure) — responder
- Loid Forger (Field Systems) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to meter-reader.
- **T+13m** — Winry Rockbell was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+47m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+152m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that a network change reduced the VPN MTU to 1400, and the meter fleet's TLS handshake packets were silently fragmented and dropped.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Clamp MSS on the VPN concentrator (owner: Yor Forger)
- [ ] Add fleet connectivity canary (owner: Winry Rockbell)

title: Postmortem INC-2119: Duplicate outage push notifications
text:
# Postmortem INC-2119: Duplicate outage push notifications

**Date:** 2026-03-28 · **Severity:** SEV3 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Usopp (Customer Platform)

The following is the incident review for INC-2119, duplicate outage push notifications. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

Impact was contained to internal teams; no customer-facing degradation was observed. Time to detect was 11 minutes; time to mitigate was 95 minutes; full resolution took 320 minutes.

## Affected services

- **outage-notifier** (owned by Customer Platform): sends outage notifications via email, SMS and push

## Responders

- Usopp (Customer Platform) — incident lead
- Asuka Langley (Customer Platform) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to outage-notifier.
- **T+11m** — Usopp was paged and declared SEV3. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+95m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+320m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the push provider returned 5xx after successfully delivering, and our retry logic had no deduplication window.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Cross-team communication was fast and focused.

## Action items

- [ ] Add 10-minute deduplication window keyed by notification id (owner: Usopp)
- [ ] Treat provider 5xx-after-delivery as success (owner: Asuka Langley)

title: Postmortem INC-2120: Billing engine OOM during quarterly reconciliation
text:
# Postmortem INC-2120: Billing engine OOM during quarterly reconciliation

**Date:** 2026-04-12 · **Severity:** SEV1 · **Status:** Resolved
**Incident lead:** Light Yagami (Payments & Billing)

The following is the incident review for INC-2120, billing engine OOM during quarterly reconciliation. It was compiled from the incident channel and on-call notes.

## Impact

The incident was visible to customers and triggered inbound support contacts. Time to detect was 19 minutes; time to mitigate was 64 minutes; full resolution took 133 minutes.

## Affected services

- **billing-engine** (owned by Payments & Billing): computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans
- **reporting-service** (owned by Data Platform): generates regulatory and internal reports

## Responders

- Light Yagami (Payments & Billing) — incident lead
- Olivier Armstrong (Data Platform) — responder
- Kakashi Hatake (Core Infrastructure) — responder

## Timeline

- **T+0** — Monitoring detected anomalous behaviour related to billing-engine.
- **T+19m** — Light Yagami was paged and declared SEV1. An incident channel was opened.
- **T+64m** — Mitigation applied; impact reduced while investigation continued.
- **T+133m** — Root cause confirmed and fix deployed. Incident closed.

## Root cause

The root cause was that the reconciliation job loaded the full quarter of invoice lines into memory instead of streaming, exceeding the 8GB pod limit.

## What went well

- Paging worked and the right people were in the channel within minutes.
- Rollback procedures were up to date.

## Action items

- [ ] Stream reconciliation instead of loading in memory (owner: Kakashi Hatake)
- [ ] Alert at 80% of pod memory limit (owner: Kakashi Hatake)

title: ADR-001: Adopt Kafka as the event backbone for grid telemetry
text:
# ADR-001: Adopt Kafka as the event backbone for grid telemetry

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2024-07-01 · **Author:** Motoko Kusanagi (Grid Operations)
**Primary service:** telemetry-ingest

## Context and decision

Substation sensor volume is growing 40% year over year and the previous RabbitMQ setup could not provide replayability. We evaluated Kafka, Pulsar and Kinesis. Kafka was selected for its mature ecosystem, exactly-once semantics within a partition, and the team's operational familiarity. telemetry-ingest publishes raw readings to the `grid.telemetry.raw` topic; grid-monitor and data-lake-sync consume independently. Consequence: consumer group rebalances become an operational concern and must be monitored.

title: ADR-002: Split billing-engine from payment-gateway
text:
# ADR-002: Split billing-engine from payment-gateway

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2024-08-30 · **Author:** Nico Robin (Payments & Billing)
**Primary service:** billing-engine

## Context and decision

Historically invoice computation and payment execution lived in a single 'billing' monolith. Deploys of tariff logic risked payment processing, and PCI scope covered the entire codebase. We decided to split into billing-engine (invoice computation, tariff application) and payment-gateway (PSP integration, settlement). billing-engine calls payment-gateway over a versioned internal API. Consequence: PCI scope shrinks to payment-gateway alone, but we accept a synchronous dependency from billing-engine to payment-gateway for payment initiation.

title: ADR-003: Standardize authentication on auth-service with short-lived JWTs
text:
# ADR-003: Standardize authentication on auth-service with short-lived JWTs

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2024-10-09 · **Author:** Erza Scarlet (Security & Identity)
**Primary service:** auth-service

## Context and decision

Each platform previously handled sessions differently. We standardize on auth-service issuing 15-minute JWTs with rotating signing keys published via JWKS. customer-portal, mobile-api, api-gateway and payment-gateway validate tokens locally. Consequence: key rotation becomes a coordinated event across all consumers; a stale JWKS cache anywhere causes validation failures.

title: ADR-004: Adopt Avro with a schema registry for meter events
text:
# ADR-004: Adopt Avro with a schema registry for meter events

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2024-12-28 · **Author:** Winry Rockbell (Field Systems)
**Primary service:** meter-reader

## Context and decision

Meter firmware evolves independently of backend services and past encoding changes have broken downstream consumers. We adopt Avro with a central schema registry and backward-compatibility enforcement. meter-reader registers schemas on deploy; data-lake-sync and billing-engine resolve schemas at read time. Consequence: schema changes that break compatibility are rejected in CI rather than discovered in production.

title: ADR-005: Move demand forecasting to a feature-store architecture
text:
# ADR-005: Move demand forecasting to a feature-store architecture

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2025-02-06 · **Author:** Frieren (Forecasting & AI)
**Primary service:** forecast-service

## Context and decision

Forecast features were computed ad hoc inside training scripts, causing training/serving skew. We introduce a feature store fed by data-lake-sync; forecast-service reads identical features at training and inference time. Consequence: data-lake-sync becomes a hard dependency of the forecasting stack and its freshness SLO must be tightened to 30 minutes.

title: ADR-006: Introduce api-gateway as the single edge for external traffic
text:
# ADR-006: Introduce api-gateway as the single edge for external traffic

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2024-11-18 · **Author:** Misato Katsuragi (Core Infrastructure)
**Primary service:** api-gateway

## Context and decision

External clients previously reached services through three separate load balancers with inconsistent TLS and rate-limiting configuration. We introduce api-gateway as the single edge: TLS termination, rate limiting, and routing to customer-portal and mobile-api. Consequence: api-gateway becomes a critical single point of failure and must run across three availability zones.

title: ADR-007: Use outbox pattern for outage notifications
text:
# ADR-007: Use outbox pattern for outage notifications

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2025-03-28 · **Author:** Usopp (Customer Platform)
**Primary service:** outage-notifier

## Context and decision

Notifications were previously fired directly from grid-monitor event handlers, and a notifier outage meant lost notifications. We adopt the transactional outbox: grid-monitor writes notification intents to its database; outage-notifier polls the outbox and handles delivery, retries and deduplication. Consequence: at-least-once delivery becomes the contract, and deduplication must be handled downstream.

title: ADR-008: Stream month-end reporting instead of batch loading
text:
# ADR-008: Stream month-end reporting instead of batch loading

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2026-02-21 · **Author:** Olivier Armstrong (Data Platform)
**Primary service:** reporting-service

## Context and decision

Month-end regulatory exports have repeatedly hit statement timeouts and memory limits as invoice volume grows (see INC-2116 and INC-2120). We move reporting-service to cursor-based streaming over billing data with checkpointing. Consequence: exports become resumable and memory-bounded, at the cost of a more complex failure model.

title: ADR-009: Adopt OpenTelemetry across all services
text:
# ADR-009: Adopt OpenTelemetry across all services

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2025-05-17 · **Author:** Kakashi Hatake (Core Infrastructure)
**Primary service:** api-gateway

## Context and decision

Incident investigations regularly stall while correlating logs across services with inconsistent formats. We adopt OpenTelemetry tracing with W3C trace context propagated from api-gateway through every internal call. Consequence: every service owner must instrument their service; Core Infrastructure provides a shared SDK wrapper.

title: ADR-010: Isolate corporate payments onto a dedicated PSP route
text:
# ADR-010: Isolate corporate payments onto a dedicated PSP route

**Status:** Accepted · **Date:** 2026-04-02 · **Author:** Roronoa Zoro (Payments & Billing)
**Primary service:** payment-gateway

## Context and decision

Corporate invoices routinely exceed the consumer PSP's per-transaction limit (see INC-2117). We route payments above €50,000 to a dedicated corporate PSP with bank-transfer rails. payment-gateway selects the route based on amount and account type. Consequence: two PSP integrations must be maintained, with separate settlement reconciliation.

title: Runbook: telemetry-ingest — Consumer lag on grid.telemetry.raw
text:
# Runbook: telemetry-ingest — Consumer lag on grid.telemetry.raw

**Service:** telemetry-ingest · **Owning team:** Grid Operations · **Last reviewed:** 2026-05-02

## Service summary

telemetry-ingest ingests real-time sensor data from grid substations.

## Scenario: Consumer lag on grid.telemetry.raw

Check the consumer group status first. If partitions are unassigned, restart one consumer pod at a time and watch reassignment. Never restart all pods simultaneously — a full rebalance under load extends the lag. If lag exceeds 15 minutes, notify Grid Operations on-call because grid-monitor alerting fidelity degrades.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Grid Operations on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: grid-monitor — Alert storm triage
text:
# Runbook: grid-monitor — Alert storm triage

**Service:** grid-monitor · **Owning team:** Grid Operations · **Last reviewed:** 2025-06-30

## Service summary

grid-monitor real-time monitoring and alerting for grid health.

## Scenario: Alert storm triage

If alert volume exceeds 50/minute, first verify thresholds against the production config repository — staging values have leaked before. Silence by alert class, never globally. Escalate to the Grid Operations tech lead if substation-level alerts persist after threshold verification.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Grid Operations on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: dispatch-optimizer — Stale forecast input
text:
# Runbook: dispatch-optimizer — Stale forecast input

**Service:** dispatch-optimizer · **Owning team:** Grid Operations · **Last reviewed:** 2025-11-18

## Service summary

dispatch-optimizer optimizes power dispatch across generation assets.

## Scenario: Stale forecast input

The optimizer refuses to run if the forecast file is older than 6 hours, and falls back to the last valid dispatch plan. Verify forecast-service published today's file to the shared bucket; if not, page Forecasting & AI. Do not hand-edit dispatch plans without sign-off from Grid Operations.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Grid Operations on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: customer-portal — Login failure spike
text:
# Runbook: customer-portal — Login failure spike

**Service:** customer-portal · **Owning team:** Customer Platform · **Last reviewed:** 2025-08-25

## Service summary

customer-portal customer-facing web application for accounts and usage.

## Scenario: Login failure spike

Login failures above 5% are almost always upstream: check auth-service JWKS freshness first, then api-gateway 5xx rates. The portal caches JWKS for 10 minutes; a forced refresh endpoint exists at /internal/jwks/refresh. If failures correlate with an auth-service deploy, roll back auth-service, not the portal.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Customer Platform on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: mobile-api — Stale usage data reports
text:
# Runbook: mobile-api — Stale usage data reports

**Service:** mobile-api · **Owning team:** Customer Platform · **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-23

## Service summary

mobile-api backend-for-frontend serving the mobile apps.

## Scenario: Stale usage data reports

Usage summaries are cached; check the cache TTL configuration before investigating pipelines. If data-lake-sync freshness lags, the mobile app shows yesterday's usage — this is expected degraded mode, communicate it in the status page rather than restarting services.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Customer Platform on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: outage-notifier — Duplicate or misdirected notifications
text:
# Runbook: outage-notifier — Duplicate or misdirected notifications

**Service:** outage-notifier · **Owning team:** Customer Platform · **Last reviewed:** 2026-03-06

## Service summary

outage-notifier sends outage notifications via email, SMS and push.

## Scenario: Duplicate or misdirected notifications

Stop the world first: pause outbox polling via the /internal/pause endpoint. Verify recipient resolution keys (must be account id) and the deduplication window. Misdirected notifications are a privacy incident — page Security & Identity immediately in addition to the service owner.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Customer Platform on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: payment-gateway — PSP timeout spike
text:
# Runbook: payment-gateway — PSP timeout spike

**Service:** payment-gateway · **Owning team:** Payments & Billing · **Last reviewed:** 2025-10-29

## Service summary

payment-gateway processes card and bank payments with external PSPs.

## Scenario: PSP timeout spike

Check connection pool saturation dashboards before anything else — pool exhaustion looks identical to PSP-side degradation. If the PSP status page confirms an incident on their side, enable the payment retry queue and inform Payments & Billing lead. Never disable idempotency checks to 'push payments through'.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Payments & Billing on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: billing-engine — Invoice batch failure
text:
# Runbook: billing-engine — Invoice batch failure

**Service:** billing-engine · **Owning team:** Payments & Billing · **Last reviewed:** 2025-11-26

## Service summary

billing-engine computes invoices from usage data and tariff plans.

## Scenario: Invoice batch failure

Identify whether the failure is pre- or post-write. Pre-write failures can simply be re-run. Post-write failures must never be blindly retried — verify idempotency keys are present for the batch, otherwise duplicate invoices are created (see INC-2101). Quarterly reconciliation jobs must be run with streaming mode enabled.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Payments & Billing on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: tariff-service — Tariff change validation
text:
# Runbook: tariff-service — Tariff change validation

**Service:** tariff-service · **Owning team:** Payments & Billing · **Last reviewed:** 2025-12-31

## Service summary

tariff-service manages tariff plans and pricing rules.

## Scenario: Tariff change validation

Every tariff change requires an explicit effective_date — the API rejects omissions since INC-2110. Preview the recalculation scope with the dry-run endpoint before applying. Changes touching closed invoices require Payments & Billing lead approval.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Payments & Billing on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: data-lake-sync — Dead-letter queue growth
text:
# Runbook: data-lake-sync — Dead-letter queue growth

**Service:** data-lake-sync · **Owning team:** Data Platform · **Last reviewed:** 2026-04-03

## Service summary

data-lake-sync syncs operational data into the analytics lake.

## Scenario: Dead-letter queue growth

DLQ depth above 1,000 events indicates schema rejection. Compare the producing service's registered schema version against what data-lake-sync resolves. Replay from the DLQ only after the schema mismatch is fixed; replaying rejected events without a fix loops them straight back.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Data Platform on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: reporting-service — Month-end export failure
text:
# Runbook: reporting-service — Month-end export failure

**Service:** reporting-service · **Owning team:** Data Platform · **Last reviewed:** 2025-12-30

## Service summary

reporting-service generates regulatory and internal reports.

## Scenario: Month-end export failure

Exports are checkpointed; always resume, never restart from scratch. Check the checkpoint table for the last completed cursor. If the export is close to the regulatory deadline, notify Data Platform lead — a manual extract procedure exists but requires four-eyes approval.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Data Platform on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: forecast-service — Implausible forecast values
text:
# Runbook: forecast-service — Implausible forecast values

**Service:** forecast-service · **Owning team:** Forecasting & AI · **Last reviewed:** 2025-07-16

## Service summary

forecast-service predicts energy demand using ML models.

## Scenario: Implausible forecast values

The service enforces physical bounds on predictions since INC-2106; if bounds violations spike, the model is likely consuming corrupted features. Freeze model promotion, pin the previous model version, and open a data-quality investigation with Data Platform.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Forecasting & AI on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: meter-reader — Fleet disconnect wave
text:
# Runbook: meter-reader — Fleet disconnect wave

**Service:** meter-reader · **Owning team:** Field Systems · **Last reviewed:** 2026-03-23

## Service summary

meter-reader collects readings from smart meters in the field.

## Scenario: Fleet disconnect wave

Distinguish network from firmware: a synchronized disconnect across regions after a network change points to MTU/MSS issues (see INC-2118); region-by-region degradation after a rollout points to firmware. The fleet canary dashboard shows handshake success rates per region.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Field Systems on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: auth-service — Token validation latency
text:
# Runbook: auth-service — Token validation latency

**Service:** auth-service · **Owning team:** Security & Identity · **Last reviewed:** 2026-03-20

## Service summary

auth-service authentication and token issuance for all platforms.

## Scenario: Token validation latency

Check certificate chain validity first — an expired intermediate certificate causes platform-wide latency (see INC-2111). Certificate expiry alerts fire at 30/14/7 days; if you are reading this during an incident, someone ignored three alerts. Key rotations must follow the JWKS propagation checklist.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Security & Identity on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Runbook: api-gateway — Elevated 502 responses
text:
# Runbook: api-gateway — Elevated 502 responses

**Service:** api-gateway · **Owning team:** Core Infrastructure · **Last reviewed:** 2025-08-27

## Service summary

api-gateway edge routing, rate limiting and TLS termination.

## Scenario: Elevated 502 responses

Compare upstream keepalive and LB idle timeouts before scaling anything — mismatched timeouts produce 502s that look like capacity problems (see INC-2107). The gateway runs in three availability zones; verify zone health before failing over.

## Escalation

Primary escalation is the Core Infrastructure on-call rotation. For customer-data exposure of any kind, additionally page Security & Identity without waiting for confirmation.

title: Slack thread #incident-2102 (2025-08-06)
text:
# Slack thread — #incident-2102 (2025-08-06)

**Roronoa Zoro:** seeing PSP timeouts climb again, same shape as yesterday evening
**Misato Katsuragi:** pool saturation graph is pegged at 20/20 connections. that's our side, not theirs
**Nico Robin:** raising the pool now. also queuing a proper fix — we should reject instead of queue when saturated
**Roronoa Zoro:** confirmed recovery at 19:42. writing the postmortem tomorrow, action items already obvious

title: Slack thread #payments-billing (2025-12-03)
text:
# Slack thread — #payments-billing (2025-12-03)

**Light Yagami:** reminder: the effective_date guard is live. tariff calls without it now 400
**Mikasa Ackerman:** already caught one integration that was omitting it 🎉
**Nico Robin:** good. INC-2110 must never happen again — recalculating closed invoices was our worst week this year

title: Slack thread #platform-eng (2025-12-15)
text:
# Slack thread — #platform-eng (2025-12-15)

**Franky:** post-INC-2111 we now monitor cert expiry at 30/14/7 days across everything the gateway touches
**Kakashi Hatake:** the irony of an *identity* provider going down because of an expired intermediate cert
**Misato Katsuragi:** 800ms extra on every request platform-wide. cheapest possible lesson would have been reading the alert email
**Erza Scarlet:** to be fair the alert went to a mailbox that was decommissioned in the org migration. fixed now — alerts page on-call directly

title: Slack thread #forecasting (2026-01-28)
text:
# Slack thread — #forecasting (2026-01-28)

**Kurisu Makise:** dispatch got yesterday's forecast again — pipeline ordering, publication ran before inference finished
**Frieren:** adding an explicit dependency between the steps. also making the optimizer fail closed on stale files
**Levi Ackerman:** grid ops appreciates it. we dispatched against stale numbers for 6 hours before anyone noticed

title: Slack thread #data-platform (2025-11-14)
text:
# Slack thread — #data-platform (2025-11-14)

**L Lawliet:** DLQ hit 40k events silently. meter-reader added a field and our strict avro reader said no
**Bulma:** the registry migration (ADR-004) fixes exactly this — compatibility check happens in their CI, not our runtime
**Shikamaru Nara:** also adding DLQ depth alerting. 40k silent rejections is embarrassing

title: Slack thread #field-systems (2026-03-14)
text:
# Slack thread — #field-systems (2026-03-14)

**Winry Rockbell:** fleet disconnect root cause confirmed: VPN MTU dropped to 1400, TLS handshakes fragmented and dropped
**Yor Forger:** clamping MSS on the concentrator. networking change reviews now include the meter fleet checklist
**Loid Forger:** adding a per-region handshake canary so next time we see it in minutes, not hours

title: Slack thread #customer-platform (2025-10-30)
text:
# Slack thread — #customer-platform (2025-10-30)

**Nami:** postal-code cache keys. we sent outage notifications to *neighbours* of affected customers
**Usopp:** every per-customer cache is now keyed by account id, checked in CI with a lint rule
**Erza Scarlet:** security review sign-off done. classifying as privacy near-miss, regulator notification not required this time

title: Slack thread #billing-oncall (2026-04-13)
text:
# Slack thread — #billing-oncall (2026-04-13)

**Light Yagami:** reconciliation OOM'd again, 8GB pod limit, full quarter in memory
**Olivier Armstrong:** ADR-008 streaming work covers this. reporting-service is moving to cursor-based export, billing reconciliation should adopt the same pattern
**Kakashi Hatake:** bumping the pod to 16GB as a stopgap tonight so the quarter closes, but the fix is streaming, not RAM

title: Slack thread #grid-ops (2025-12-24)
text:
# Slack thread — #grid-ops (2025-12-24)

**Edward Elric:** alert storm was a config leak — staging threshold 0.1 went to prod, should be 0.7
**Spike Spiegel:** separating the config repos per ADR discussion. also adding config diff to deploy approvals
**Motoko Kusanagi:** good catch. 300 pages in one night is how you burn out an on-call rotation

title: Slack thread #security (2026-01-03)
text:
# Slack thread — #security (2026-01-03)

**Lelouch Lamperouge:** bank rejected the settlement file — they moved to ISO 20022 on a date that lived in a spreadsheet
**Roronoa Zoro:** partner contract dates are going into the service catalog with automated reminders. spreadsheets are where deadlines go to die

title: Slack thread #mobile (2026-01-13)
text:
# Slack thread — #mobile (2026-01-13)

**Asuka Langley:** stale usage data reports — cache TTL was raised to 24h in a cost change, nobody added invalidation
**Shikamaru Nara:** wiring invalidation to ingestion events now. TTL changes above 1h now need review

title: Slack thread #eng-all (2026-05-12)
text:
# Slack thread — #eng-all (2026-05-12)

**Misato Katsuragi:** OpenTelemetry rollout is at 12 of 15 services. holdouts: dispatch-optimizer, tariff-service, meter-reader
**Winry Rockbell:** meter-reader lands next sprint, embedded constraints made the SDK wrapper tricky
**Kakashi Hatake:** once we're at 15/15, incident correlation stops being archaeology. looking forward to it

title: NordWind Energy — Engineering Overview
text:
# NordWind Energy — Engineering Overview

NordWind Energy is a fictional Northern European power utility serving 2.4 million customers. Engineering is organised into eight teams: Grid Operations, Customer Platform, Payments & Billing, Data Platform, Core Infrastructure, Forecasting & AI, Field Systems, Security & Identity. Each service has exactly one owning team, and cross-team changes go through the architecture review process documented in our ADRs. The platform runs on Kubernetes across three availability zones, with Kafka as the event backbone and PostgreSQL as the default operational store.

title: Service Catalog — Customer Domain
text:
# Service Catalog — Customer Domain

The customer domain consists of customer-portal (the web application), mobile-api (backend-for-frontend for the iOS and Android apps), and outage-notifier (multi-channel notifications). All customer-facing traffic enters through api-gateway. Authentication is delegated to auth-service; the portal and mobile-api never handle credentials directly. Usage data displayed to customers originates from meter-reader and flows through data-lake-sync.

title: Service Catalog — Payments Domain
text:
# Service Catalog — Payments Domain

The payments domain is owned by Payments & Billing and consists of three services. tariff-service manages pricing plans and their effective dates. billing-engine computes invoices by combining meter readings with tariff rules. payment-gateway executes payments against external PSPs and handles settlement files. The split between billing-engine and payment-gateway is documented in ADR-002 and keeps PCI scope minimal. Corporate payments above €50,000 use a dedicated PSP route per ADR-010.

title: Service Catalog — Grid Domain
text:
# Service Catalog — Grid Domain

The grid domain covers real-time operations: telemetry-ingest consumes substation sensor streams into Kafka, grid-monitor evaluates grid health and raises alerts, and dispatch-optimizer plans generation dispatch using demand forecasts. The forecasts come from forecast-service, owned by Forecasting & AI. Grid operations are safety-relevant: dispatch plan overrides always require human sign-off.

title: On-call and Incident Process
text:
# On-call and Incident Process

Every team runs a weekly on-call rotation. Incidents are classified SEV1 (customer-facing, all hands), SEV2 (significant, limited visibility) and SEV3 (internal). The incident lead is the first responder from the owning team of the most affected service. Every SEV1 and SEV2 requires a blameless postmortem within five working days. Privacy-relevant incidents additionally page Security & Identity regardless of severity.

title: New Engineer Onboarding — Week One
text:
# New Engineer Onboarding — Week One

Welcome to NordWind. In week one you will: get access to the service catalog and this documentation set; shadow your team's on-call; read the three most recent postmortems in your domain; and deploy a small change behind a feature flag. Our engineering culture values blameless reviews, written decisions (ADRs), and boring technology chosen deliberately.

title: Data Flow — From Meter to Invoice
text:
# Data Flow — From Meter to Invoice

A meter reading travels the following path: meter-reader collects it from the field device and publishes an Avro event; data-lake-sync lands it in the analytics lake; billing-engine consumes readings for the billing period, applies tariffs from tariff-service, and produces an invoice; payment-gateway then executes payment collection. Any break in this chain shows up, eventually, as a billing anomaly — which is why meter events use schema-registry enforcement per ADR-004.

title: Observability Standards
text:
# Observability Standards

All services emit OpenTelemetry traces with W3C context propagation, structured JSON logs, and RED metrics (rate, errors, duration). Golden-signal dashboards are generated from the service catalog. Alerting policy: every page must be actionable and linked to a runbook. Alert fatigue is treated as an incident in its own right — see the grid-monitor alert storm postmortem for why.
